title: [FIRRTL][CheckCombLoops] Support aggregates with flips
body:
This is not an issue as long as this executes after LowerTypes, as it will break apart bundles with flips.  So prioritize accordingly.

Anyway, example input:

```mlir
firrtl.circuit "Check" {
  firrtl.module private @Child(in %in: !firrtl.bundle<x: uint<5>, y flip: uint<5>>, out %out: !firrtl.bundle<x: uint<5>, y flip: uint<5>>) {
    %0 = firrtl.subfield %out[x] : !firrtl.bundle<x: uint<5>, y flip: uint<5>>
    %1 = firrtl.subfield %in[x] : !firrtl.bundle<x: uint<5>, y flip: uint<5>>
    firrtl.strictconnect %0, %1 : !firrtl.uint<5>
    %2 = firrtl.subfield %out[y] : !firrtl.bundle<x: uint<5>, y flip: uint<5>>
    %3 = firrtl.subfield %in[y] : !firrtl.bundle<x: uint<5>, y flip: uint<5>>
    firrtl.strictconnect %3, %2 : !firrtl.uint<5>
  }
  firrtl.module @Check() attributes {convention = #firrtl<convention scalarized>} {
    %c_in, %c_out = firrtl.instance c @Child(in in: !firrtl.bundle<x: uint<5>, y flip: uint<5>>, out out: !firrtl.bundle<x: uint<5>, y flip: uint<5>>)
    %0 = firrtl.subfield %c_in[x] : !firrtl.bundle<x: uint<5>, y flip: uint<5>>
    %1 = firrtl.subfield %c_in[y] : !firrtl.bundle<x: uint<5>, y flip: uint<5>>
    %2 = firrtl.subfield %c_out[y] : !firrtl.bundle<x: uint<5>, y flip: uint<5>>
    firrtl.strictconnect %2, %1 : !firrtl.uint<5>
    %c5_ui3 = firrtl.constant 5 : !firrtl.uint<3>
    firrtl.connect %0, %c5_ui3 : !firrtl.uint<5>, !firrtl.uint<3>
  }
}
```

Original FIRRTL input FWIW:
```firrtl
circuit Check:
  module Child:
    input in : { x : UInt<5>, flip y : UInt<5> }
    output out : { x : UInt<5>, flip y : UInt<5> }

    out <= in

  module Check:
    inst c of Child

    c.out.y <= c.in.y
    c.in.x <= UInt(5)
```

Noticed reviewing code where Direction of port is used, but with flips there may be more to the story.
